idea-plugin.name: Whisper Speech to Text
idea-plugin.description:
Voice-to-text dictation for JetBrains IDEs using OpenAI Whisper. Speak instead of type, anywhere a caret is focused.

Features

  • Three transcription backends: local (whisper.cpp, fully offline), OpenAI Whisper API, Groq (Whisper Large V3).
  • Three dictation modes: Dictate (raw transcription), Code (spoken punctuation mapped to symbols, filler words removed), Command (formatted as a coding instruction for AI chat).
  • Inserts at the editor caret. Falls back to clipboard paste for tool windows, terminals, and external apps.
  • Multilingual: supports the languages Whisper supports (en, fr, de, es, it, pt, ja, zh, and more).
  • Local backend runs entirely on your machine. No audio leaves your computer unless you select a cloud backend.

Quick start

  1. Install sox (brew install sox) or ffmpeg for audio capture.
  2. Open any project (Whisper detects missing setup and shows a prompt). Click Run Local Setup to install whisper.cpp and a model (~150 MB), or Open Settings to configure an OpenAI/Groq API key.
  3. Press Cmd+M (macOS) or Ctrl+M (Windows/Linux) to start recording. Press again to stop and insert the transcription.

Configuration

Settings -> Tools -> Whisper. Configure backend, dictation mode, language, API keys, and recording tool.

Source code, issue tracker, and documentation: github.com/roussi/whisper
